source.expression:
$product_ids        = $wpdb->get_col(
			$wpdb->prepare(
				"SELECT post.ID
				FROM {$wpdb->posts} as post
				LEFT JOIN {$wpdb->posts} as parent ON post.post_parent = parent.ID
				WHERE
					(
						( post.post_type = 'product_variation' AND parent.post_status = 'publish'
							AND EXISTS (
								SELECT 1
								FROM {$wpdb->term_relationships} tr
								INNER JOIN {$wpdb->term_taxonomy} tt ON tr.term_taxonomy_id = tt.term_taxonomy_id
								INNER JOIN {$wpdb->terms} t ON tt.term_id = t.term_id
								WHERE tr.object_id = parent.ID AND tt.taxonomy = 'product_type' AND t.slug LIKE %s
							)
						)
					OR
						( post.post_type = 'product' AND post.post_status = 'publish' )
					)
				AND
					post.ID > %d
				ORDER BY post.ID ASC
				LIMIT %d",
				$variable_type_like,
				$this->get_last_batch_id( $batch_number ),
				$this->get_batch_size()
			)
		)

chain.expression:
$product_ids        = $wpdb->get_col(
			$wpdb->prepare(
				"SELECT post.ID
				FROM {$wpdb->posts} as post
				LEFT JOIN {$wpdb->posts} as parent ON post.post_parent = parent.ID
				WHERE
					(
						( post.post_type = 'product_variation' AND parent.post_status = 'publish'
							AND EXISTS (
								SELECT 1
								FROM {$wpdb->term_relationships} tr
								INNER JOIN {$wpdb->term_taxonomy} tt ON tr.term_taxonomy_id = tt.term_taxonomy_id
								INNER JOIN {$wpdb->terms} t ON tt.term_id = t.term_id
								WHERE tr.object_id = parent.ID AND tt.taxonomy = 'product_type' AND t.slug LIKE %s
							)
						)
					OR
						( post.post_type = 'product' AND post.post_status = 'publish' )
					)
				AND
					post.ID > %d
				ORDER BY post.ID ASC
				LIMIT %d",
				$variable_type_like,
				$this->get_last_batch_id( $batch_number ),
				$this->get_batch_size()
			)
		)

taint_flows.missing_sanitizer: esc_html() / esc_attr() not applied before output

extra.message: md5() used for hashing data. If this value is used for authentication, integrity verification, or token generation, md5 is insufficient. Use a stronger algorithm.
REST route registered without a permission_callback. This defaults to open access in older WordPress versions. Always define a permission_callback that verifies user capabilities.
